findings.evidence: New lines 44-68 prove the declarations by exactly these APIs; existing hits are `mathlib/Mathlib/Logic/Function/Iterate.lean:76: theorem iterate_add_apply`, `mathlib/Mathlib/MeasureTheory/MeasurableSpace/Basic.lean:290: theorem Measurable.iterate`, `mathlib/Mathlib/Dynamics/Ergodic/MeasurePreserving.lean:193: protected theorem iterate`, `mathlib/Mathlib/MeasureTheory/MeasurableSpace/Constructions.lean:573: theorem measurable_pi_apply`, plus TauCeti `measurable_shift` at `TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability/Basic.lean:115`.
findings.fix: Remove these public aliases and use the existing declarations directly: `Function.iterate_add_apply`, `measurable_shift.iterate r` / `Measurable.iterate`, `hν.iterate r` / `MeasurePreserving.iterate`, and `measurable_pi_apply (n + r)`.
findings.issue: `shift_iterate_add_apply`, `measurable_shift_iterate`, `MeasurePreserving.shift_iterate`, and `measurable_shifted_eval` duplicate existing generic API specialized to `shift`.

summary: Several new public declarations are direct wrappers around existing Mathlib/TauCeti API rather than reusable new path-space facts.
